title.rendered: Sun, Sand & Spending Money: How Far Will Your Pound Go This Summer?

content.rendered:
Exchange rates correct at time of publishing – but they do change frequently. For the latest rates, visit Travel FX.

As the countdown to summer begins, British holidaymakers are locking in their getaways—and their travel money. With exchange rates shifting daily, knowing where your pound stretches furthest can help you enjoy more cocktails, more excursions, and more unforgettable experiences.

At Travel FX, we offer market-leading rates on dozens of currencies. Here’s how far £1,000 will take you in some of this year’s most popular destinations:

Turkey – Approx. 47,119 Turkish Lira (TRY)

Rate: 1 GBP = 47.1197 TRY

Turkey continues to offer phenomenal value for British travellers. Imagine sipping Turkish tea on a sun-drenched terrace in Fethiye, exploring the markets of Istanbul, or floating over Cappadocia in a hot air balloon—all for less than you’d spend on a night out back home. From all-inclusive beach breaks to cultural city escapes, the Turkish Lira gives you a lot of bang for your buck.

Your £1,000 could cover a luxury week-long hotel stay, daily dining, excursions, and still leave room for souvenirs.

USA – Approx. 1,314 US Dollars (USD)

Rate: 1 GBP = 1.3146 USD

The USA might be pricier, but it’s packed with once-in-a-lifetime experiences. Think rooftop bars in New York, road trips down the California coast, or star-spotting in Los Angeles. Whether you’re going theme park mad in Florida or hiking in the Grand Canyon, every dollar counts.

With £1,000, you can cover park passes, hire a car, or enjoy a Broadway show—plus a few shopping sprees.

Eurozone – Approx. 1,141 Euros (EUR)

Rate: 1 GBP = 1.1414 EUR

Europe is a classic for a reason. Stroll the cobbled streets of Barcelona with a gelato in hand, sip wine in a Parisian café, or island-hop across the Greek isles. The euro may not stretch as far as it used to, but savvy travellers can still find amazing deals—especially if you order your euros in advance at a great rate.

£1,000 could mean sunset dinners, beach days, cultural tours, and enough left over for airport sangria.

Australia – Approx. 2,041 Australian Dollars (AUD)

Rate: 1 GBP = 2.0419 AUD

Heading Down Under? Australia may be a once-in-a-lifetime trip, but your pound still holds solid value. From snorkelling the Great Barrier Reef to wine tasting in the Barossa Valley, your AUD goes a long way when you plan smart.

£1,000 could cover domestic flights, a couple of unforgettable tours, or some top-notch dining in Sydney or Melbourne.

Poland – Approx. 4,829 Polish Zloty (PLN)

Rate: 1 GBP = 4.8293 PLN

Poland is one of Europe’s hidden gems. From the medieval charm of Kraków to the coastal vibes of Gdańsk, it’s a fantastic low-cost option. Accommodation, food, and drink are all budget-friendly—and you won’t be compromising on quality.

Your £1,000 will go far: think boutique stays, hearty meals, and spa treatments without the scary price tag.

Bonus Tip: Don’t Forget Our Buyback Service

Came home with leftover currency? Don’t leave it gathering dust in a drawer. Travel FX offers a hassle-free Buyback Service with highly competitive rates—often better than what you’d get at the airport or bank. Just post your unused cash and we’ll deposit the pounds directly into your bank account.

Important: Exchange rates change constantly, so don’t delay. Check today’s rate at www.travelfx.co.uk and secure the best deal for your summer travel money.

Your trip begins with a better exchange rate.

itemListElement.name: Sun, Sand & Spending Money: How Far Will Your Pound Go This Summer?

@graph.name: Travel FX Blog
@graph.description: Travel money news and tips from Travel FX

@graph.name: Lucy Reed
@graph.description: Hi! I’m Lucy, as well as writing blog posts I lead Customer Experience and UX at Travel FX. With over 15 years’ experience in the foreign exchange industry, I specialise in helping UK travellers understand exchange rates, avoid unnecessary fees, and feel confident about their travel money before they fly. Having lived both in the UK and overseas, and travelled extensively over the years, I understand first-hand how important it is to get the details right when you’re planning a trip. From managing your travel budget to choosing the right foreign currency or travel money card, small decisions can make a big difference.
